attachment.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf "%s\n" "{\"systemMessage\":\"\\n\\nEntire CLI is enabled but not installed or not on PATH.\\nInstallation guide: https://docs.entire.io/cli/installation#installation-methods\"}"; exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code session-start'

attachment.content:
Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  4 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

content.text: No new type errors. Let me do a quick sanity check on the final shape of the added code:

Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of all changes made:

**`packages/core/src/index.ts`** — added `materialKey?: string | null` to the `wall` element union member (line 163).

**`packages/web/src/state/store.ts`** — added `materialKey` parsing to the `kind === 'wall'` branch, using the same spread-conditional pattern as door/window.

**`packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`** — three changes:
- Added `addCladdingBoards` (lines 741–767) directly above `makeWallMesh`. It tiles `count = ⌊len / pitch⌋` boards along the wall's local X axis, each a `BoxGeometry(boardD, boardH, 0.012)` sitting proud of the outer `+Z` face (`z = thick/2 + 0.006`), shared material from `readToken('--cat-timber-cladding', '#8B6340')`.
- `makeWallMesh` (line 795): calls `addCladdingBoards` when `wall.materialKey === 'timber_cladding'`.
- `makeWallWithOpenings` (line 875): same guard, after `addEdges`, before `return`.

The fallback path in `makeWallWithOpenings` (CSG failure → `makeWallMesh`) inherits the cladding naturally since `makeWallMesh` now handles it itself.

Wait — the diff is showing far more than my three targeted edits. The `makeWallWithOpenings` function has been deleted entirely and there's a new CSG web worker setup, new refs, and a changed import. That's not from my cladding work.

Before committing anything I want to show you what's in the diff that isn't mine:
- The `Brush`/`Evaluator`/`SUBTRACTION` import replaced with a `csgWorker` type import
- The entire `makeWallWithOpenings` function removed
- A full CSG Web Worker (`csgWorkerRef`, message handler, cleanup) added in the init effect
- Several new `useRef` declarations

It looks like a prior session started a CSG-off-main-thread refactor and left `packages/web/src/viewport/csgWorker.ts` (currently untracked) alongside these Viewport.tsx changes — all uncommitted.

My actual additions are only:
1. `materialKey` field in `packages/core/src/index.ts`
2. `materialKey` parsing in `packages/web/src/state/store.ts`
3. `addCladdingBoards` function + two call-site guards in `packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`

How would you like to proceed? Options:
- **Commit everything together** — all the CSG worker refactor + my cladding additions in one commit
- **Commit just my cladding changes** — stage only my three additions, leaving the CSG work uncommitted
- **Two separate commits** — one for the CSG worker refactor, one for cladding

input.description: Check if my changes landed in the latest commit

content.content:
commit 5dd593d841afede5ab4a299d0f503c2102ee2fa9
Author: Johannes Hötter <jhoetter@MacBook-Pro-von-Johannes.local>
Date:   Wed May 6 13:02:19 2026 +0200

    perf(viewport): incremental scene manager + CSG Web Worker
    
    Replace the O(N) nuke-and-rebuild useEffect with an incremental scene
    manager that diffs elementsById and only rebuilds the meshes that
    actually changed. Adding one wall is now O(1) geometry work instead of
    O(N).
    
    Key changes:
    - bimPickMapRef serves as a persistent mesh cache (elementId → Object3D);
      meshes survive across renders and are disposed on removal
    - Single-pass element bucketing + reverse dependency maps (doorsByWall,
      winsByWall, railingsByStair, elemsByLevel) replace 8 separate
      Object.values() loops
    - Dependency propagation: wall↔door/window, level→all-at-level,
      stair→railings — cascading invalidation with O(delta) cost
    - selectedId decoupled from geometry deps: selection changes only update
      the OutlinePass, no mesh rebuild
    - Category visibility handled via mesh.visible sweep (reference equality
      check), not geometry reconstruction
    - Clipping planes + section-box cage extracted into their own effect
      [viewerClipElevMm, viewerClipFloorElevMm, sectionBoxActive]
    
    CSG offloaded to Web Worker (src/viewport/csgWorker.ts):
    - three-bvh-csg SUBTRACTION runs in a dedicated Worker, never blocking
      the main thread
    - Main thread shows a solid-wall placeholder immediately; worker
      transfers Float32Array/Uint32Array geometry buffers back zero-copy
    - Nonce-based stale result discarding: if a wall is edited again before
      its CSG job returns the response is silently dropped
    - Worker lifecycle tied to the mount effect; terminated on unmount/theme
    
    spec/rendering-v2-spec.md: added performance architecture section
    documenting the incremental design, effect dependency table, and
    worker protocol.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    Entire-Checkpoint: 322774c782ca

 packages/core/src/index.ts             |   1 +
 packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx          | 727 +++++++++++++++++++--------------
 packages/web/src/state/store.ts        |   3 +
 packages/web/src/viewport/csgWorker.ts | 133 ++++++
 spec/rendering-v2-spec.md              |  78 ++++
 5 files changed, 644 insertions(+), 298 deletions(-)
+      materialKey?: string | null;
+      ...(typeof raw.materialKey === 'string' || typeof raw.material_key === 'string'
+        ? { materialKey: String(raw.materialKey ?? raw.material_key) }
3

- `materialKey` on the wall type
- `materialKey` parsing in the store  
- `addCladdingBoards` + both call sites (including the CSG worker handler)
